bids.reasoning: At t=0.00 there is no time pressure to concede, so I am targeting my maximum-utility bid. The opponent’s first offer gives me the lowest value on every issue, and their message is just pressure without any evidence of concession, so I will counter firmly with my preferred values.

I am targeting a very high-utility bid because it is still extremely early at t=0.02, while making a small concession on my lowest-weight issue to avoid simply repeating my prior bid. The opponent has twice offered all valueD, which is the lowest-utility valid bid for me, and their messages are aggressive but not backed by any numerical concession yet.

I am still targeting a high utility because it is very early in the negotiation, but I am making a clearer concession than my prior bid by moving on the lower-weight issues first. The opponent has not conceded at all in their offers, repeatedly proposing my lowest-value package, so I am treating their messages as pressure tactics rather than evidence of a real constraint. This bid preserves my most important issues while offering movement on issueC and issueD.

I am targeting a high utility around 0.893 because it is still very early in the negotiation and there is no need to make large concessions yet. The opponent has only slightly improved their offer by moving issueA from valueD to valueC, while their messages are aggressive cheap talk, so I will concede mainly on the lowest-weight issueD and hold firm on issueA and issueB.

I am targeting a high-utility bid around 0.866 because it is still very early at t=0.08, so there is no need to make large concessions yet. The opponent’s messages are aggressive cheap talk, but their actual bids show only a limited concession on issueA while holding valueD elsewhere, so I will concede further on my lowest-weight issueD while preserving my high-weight issueA and issueB.

I am targeting a still-strong utility of about 0.814, since it is early at t=0.10 and there is no need to concede toward the opponent’s very low offers yet. The opponent’s actual offers have only improved from all valueD to issueA:valueC while holding the rest at valueD, so despite their aggressive messages, the numbers show limited concession; I will make a modest additional concession on the lower-weight issueC while holding my highest-weight issues A and B.

I am targeting a still-strong utility around 0.76 because it is early in the negotiation, but I want to show real movement without giving up the highest-weight issues. The opponent has only conceded on issueA so far while holding issueB/issueC/issueD at valueD, so I am conceding further on lower-weight issueC and keeping issueA and issueB at my top values despite their aggressive messages.

At t=0.14 it is still early, so I am targeting a high-utility bid around 0.76 rather than making deep concessions. The opponent has repeatedly held issueB/issueC/issueD at valueD while moving issueA from valueD to valueB, so I will acknowledge movement on issueA and keep firmness on the more important issueB while using lower-weight issues for tradeoffs; their aggressive messages are cheap talk but consistent with wanting more movement on issueA/issueB.

I am targeting a utility around 0.73, which is a meaningful concession from my earlier high-utility bids while still protecting the most important issueA. At t=0.16 there is still substantial time left, so I should not collapse toward the opponent’s very low-utility offers; their bids have shown limited and inconsistent concession, and their messages are mostly pressure tactics. Since they specifically objected to issueB, I am moving issueB to valueB while keeping a compensating gain on lower-priority issueC.

I am targeting a utility around 0.73, since it is still early at t=0.18 and there is no need to concede down near the opponent’s low-utility offers yet. The opponent has made only limited concessions, mostly moving on issueA earlier and recently issueB, while their messages are aggressive but not binding; I will hold firm on high-weight issueA while offering movement on issueB, issueC, and issueD.

I am targeting a moderately high utility around 0.68, since we are still early at t=0.20 but the opponent has started making meaningful concessions, especially on issueA. Their latest valid offer gives me issueA:valueA but holds the other issues at valueD, so I will keep my highest-weight issueA and concede further on lower-weight issueC and issueD while not dropping issueB all the way yet.

At t=0.22 it is still early enough to target a solid utility while making a visible concession from my prior stance. The opponent’s offers have mostly stayed very unfavorable to me, though they briefly conceded issueA to valueA and have signaled strong pressure on issueA/issueB; I will hold issueA, concede issueB further, and keep lower-weight issues already favorable to them.

I am targeting a still-strong utility around the high 0.6 range because it is only t=0.24, so there is no need to collapse to the opponent’s low-utility offers yet. The opponent has made some concessions on issueA and issueB but remains very demanding on issueC and issueD; I will concede mainly on the lower-weight issues while keeping reasonable value on the more important issueA and issueB.

I am targeting a moderate utility around 0.57, which is a meaningful concession from my prior offers while still protecting my highest-weight issues. At t=0.26 it is still early enough not to accept the opponent’s very low-utility offers, but their bids suggest they care strongly about pushing issueC and issueD to valueD and have recently moved on issueB, so I concede the lower-weight issues first and offer a compromise on issueA/issueB.

I am targeting a utility around 0.60: still protective of my highest-weight issueA, but with meaningful concessions on issueB, issueC, and issueD. At t=0.28 there is some time pressure but it is not yet late enough to drop to the opponent’s best prior offer; their messages are aggressive cheap talk, while their actual offers are erratic and not consistently improving for me.

At t=0.30 I am targeting a moderate concession around 0.55 utility, since there is still time but the opponent has begun moving slightly from all-valueD offers. Their offers and messages suggest they are pressing hardest on issueA and issueB, so I concede there while holding a reasonable middle position and matching their recent movement on issueC/issueD.

I am targeting a moderate utility around 0.57: below my earlier high-utility demands but still clearly above the opponent’s latest offer utility to me. At t=0.32 there is pressure to make real concessions, so I accept their movement on issueB and keep issueC/issueD low for me, while still holding issueA at valueB because it is my most important issue. The opponent’s messages are aggressive cheap talk, but their offers show some actual concession on issueB, so I am responding to that behavior rather than the rhetoric.

At t=0.34 I am targeting a mid-game concession around 0.55 utility for me, still above the opponent’s recent offers to me but more flexible than my earlier high-utility bids. The opponent’s actual bids have slowly improved on issueA/issueB/issueC but remain low for my utility, while their messages are mostly pressure tactics; I will concede on issueB and issueC while keeping issueA at valueB rather than dropping further on my highest-weight issue.

I am targeting a moderate-utility bid around 0.49, which is a real concession from my prior offer while still preserving value on the highest-weight issueA. At t=0.36 there is still time, but the opponent has persistently rejected high-utility bids and has shown some movement on issueA/issueB, so I am conceding more on lower-weight issueC and issueD while offering issueB at valueC.
